indication: Relapsed Metastatic Germ Cell Tumours after failure of first line therapy

pre_medications.special_instructions: The last bag of mesna on day 5 may be replaced by oral mesna 400mg/m² (rounded to nearest 400mg capsule) at 0, 2 and 6 hours after end of ifosfamide infusion

pre_medications.special_instructions: Pre-cisplatin hydration. Post hydration incorporated as part of ifosfamide/mesna administration. Consider furosemide 40mg oral or IV for fluid overload.

pre_medications.special_instructions: 125mg day 1, 80mg days 2 and 3

dose_modifications.notes: Stop ifosfamide. Methylene blue 50mg QDS IV in 100ml NaCl 0.9% over 30 minutes can be used to prevent or treat ifosfamide-induced encephalopathy.

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Delay treatment for 3 days at a time if haematological criteria not met. Restart as soon as parameters are met. Dose delays preferred over dose reductions. Cisplatin does not require haematological dose reduction.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es (magnesium, phosphate and calcium) prior to each cycle
Serum albumin prior to each cycle
EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance prior to each cycle

Fluid balance monitoring every four hours the day of and the day after ifosfamide administration - urine output should be maintained above 100ml/hour
AFP, HCG on day 1 of the cycle
special_warnings: INPATIENT REGIMEN - This is an inpatient regimen. All supportive and take home medication not on Aria must be prescribed on the inpatient chart or general electronic prescribing system.
This is a potentially curative regimen. All dose reductions and delays should be discussed with the relevant consultant.
Aria is set to dose cap all regimens at 2.4m². This regimen must NOT be capped. Please override any doses that are capped.
Paclitaxel is a vesicant; ifosfamide and mesna are neutral; cisplatin is an exfoliant.

Risk factors for CNS toxicity with ifosfamide include low albumin, renal impairment, prior cisplatin, poor performance status, CNS tumour, bulky pelvic disease, concomitant psychotropic drugs and younger age.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
Growth factor support (filgrastim/lenograstim/pegfilgrastim) starting day 7 according to local policy.

indication: Ipilimumab in combination with nivolumab is indicated for the first line treatment of unresectable or locally advanced intermediate or poor risk renal cell adenocarcinoma (RCC) which has either a clear cell component or papillary where the patient has intermediate or poor risk advanced renal cell carcinoma as assessed by IMDC and is completely treatment naive for systemic therapy for locally advanced/metastatic RCC or has only received prior systemic therapy in the context of clinical trials investigating adjuvant therapies in RCC

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer FIRST before ipilimumab. Allow 30 minutes between both agents. Administer via 0.2-1.2 micron low protein binding filter. Ensure patient has been given a nivolumab patient alert card. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for immunotherapy.
Administer AFTER nivolumab with 30-minute gap. Administer using a low protein binding filter. Final concentration should be between 1-4mg/ml. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for immunotherapy.

pre_medications.timing: when required for infusion related reactions
pre_medications.special_instructions: For treatment of infusion related reactions only

pre_medications.timing: when required for infusion related reactions
pre_medications.special_instructions: For treatment of infusion related reactions only

pre_medications.timing: when required for infusion related reactions
pre_medications.special_instructions: Maximum dose 4g per 24 hours. There should be 4 hours between doses.

dose_modifications.notes: Dose reductions NOT permitted. Both drugs must be delayed until treatment can resume. Dose delay criteria apply for all drug-related adverse events regardless of attribution.
Withhold treatment and administer corticosteroids. May re-start when LFTs remain at NCI-CTC grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

dose_modifications.condition: AST/ALT ≥5xULN or bilirubin >3xULN; or hepatic injury not improving to grade 1 within 12 weeks; or corticosteroid dose cannot be reduced to ≤10mg prednisolone per day within 12 weeks; or any grade 3+ reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue both agents

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 or 3 serum creatinine elevation
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold both agents and initiate corticosteroids. Resume when reaction remains at NCI-CTC grade 1 or below following tapering.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 4 serum creatinine elevation; or not improving to grade 1 within 12 weeks; or recurrent grade 3 reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue. Initiate corticosteroids at 1-2mg/kg/day methylprednisolone equivalents.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 diarrhoea, colitis, peritoneal signs, bowel perforation, ileus or fever
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue. Initiate high-dose IV corticosteroids.
Permanently discontinue both agents

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 4 rash or grade 3 pruritus
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue both agents

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific haematological thresholds stated; little need to adjust for haematological toxicity. Treatment breaks of up to 12 weeks beyond expected cycle length allowed. Current funding precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer. Monotherapy nivolumab follows combination phase: 240mg every 14 days (24 cycles) OR 480mg every 28 days (12 cycles).

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and then before each administration (cycle) or when clinically indicated
special_warnings: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for ipilimumab or nivolumab (immunotherapy). Delay or discontinue only.
Nivolumab must be administered FIRST during the combination phase; allow 30 minutes between both agents.

After 4 cycles of combination therapy, transition to nivolumab monotherapy: either 240mg every 14 days (24 cycles set in ARIA, starting 3 weeks after combination therapy) or 480mg every 28 days (12 cycles set in ARIA, starting 6 weeks after combination therapy).
Treatment continues until loss of clinical benefit, excessive toxicity or patient choice. There is no stopping rule as to maximum treatment duration of nivolumab monotherapy.
Current funding approval precludes further treatment after an interruption of 12 weeks or longer.

Systemic corticosteroids before starting ipilimumab should be avoided due to potential interference with pharmacodynamic activity. Can be used after starting to treat immune-related adverse reactions.
IMDC favourable risk disease did worse with this combination; ipilimumab with nivolumab combination is not licensed in the favourable risk population.
Formal medical review to assess tolerability scheduled at start of 3rd 3-weekly cycle and regularly thereafter.

drugs.special_instructions: Administer FIRST before ipilimumab. Allow 30 minutes between both agents. Administer via 0.2-1.2 micron low protein binding filter. Ensure patient has been given a nivolumab patient alert card. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for immunotherapy.
drugs.notes: every 21 days (cycles 1-4 combination phase) | Administer FIRST before ipilimumab. Allow 30 minutes between both agents. Administer via 0.2-1.2 micron low protein binding filter. Ensure patient has been given a nivolumab patient alert card. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for immunotherapy.

drugs.special_instructions: Administer AFTER nivolumab with 30-minute gap. Administer using a low protein binding filter. Final concentration should be between 1-4mg/ml. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for immunotherapy.
drugs.notes: every 21 days (cycles 1-4 combination phase) | Administer AFTER nivolumab with 30-minute gap. Administer using a low protein binding filter. Final concentration should be between 1-4mg/ml. Dose reductions are NOT permitted for immunotherapy.

indication: Castrate resistant prostate cancer; Performance status 0, 1, 2; Palliative

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose will be rounded to nearest 1mg (up if halfway). Cardiac toxicity more likely at cumulative doses exceeding 160mg/m² or 100mg/m² after previous anthracycline therapy. Mitoxantrone is a vesicant.

5mg twice a day continuously days 1-21. Take with or after food. The dose may need to be reduced gradually before stopping treatment. Patients who stop mitoxantrone may require gradual withdrawal of prednisolone.

pre_medications.timing: 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
pre_medications.special_instructions: Oral or intravenous

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Take with or after food. The dose of this medicine may need to be reduced gradually before stopping treatment.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery to 100x10⁹/L or greater then continue at full dose

dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 8mg/m² for WHO PS 0 or 1

dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment for WHO PS 2 or above with bilirubin >3xULN

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below, then reduce dose to 8mg/m² or discontinue as appropriate. Prednisolone should be continued if mitoxantrone is delayed.
Stop treatment if cardiotoxicity occurs

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to Cycle 1. Prednisolone should be continued if mitoxantrone is delayed. Prednisolone should be stopped if mitoxantrone is stopped but may require gradual dose reduction.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs, PSA and U&Es prior to day one of treatment
Consider an echocardiogram if there is a history of cardiac dysfunction
special_warnings: Mitoxantrone is a vesicant.
Cardiac toxicity is more likely at cumulative doses in excess of 160mg/m² or 100mg/m² after previous anthracycline therapy.
Prednisolone should be continued if mitoxantrone is delayed; prednisolone should be stopped if mitoxantrone is stopped but may require gradual dose reduction.
Patients who stop mitoxantrone may require gradual withdrawal of prednisolone.
If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.
Prednisolone is available as 5mg (uncoated) and 2.5mg and 5mg (enteric coated) tablets.
Prednisolone should be taken with or after food.

indication: Treatment of locally advanced or metastatic bladder cancer; WHO Performance status 0, 1, 2

chemotherapy_drugs.frequency: weekly on days 1, 8 and 15 of 28-day cycle
chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded in accordance with national dose bands (6mg/ml). Administer via non-PVC administration set with in-line 0.22 micron filter. Starting dose may be reduced to 60mg/m² in patients with poor bone marrow reserves (extensive prior treatment, bone metastasis, extensive skeletal radiation) with a view to increase to 80mg/m² if well tolerated. This is an unlicensed dosage schedule.

pre_medications.special_instructions: Antiemetic. Can also be given intravenously.

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Take home antiemetic. Dispensed as original pack on day one only.

dose_modifications.notes: Withhold treatment until recovery. Subsequent weekly dose should be decreased by one dose level (level 0: 80mg/m²/wk → level -1: 70mg/m²/wk → level -2: 60mg/m²/wk)

dose_modifications.condition: Bilirubin more than 1.5xULN and AST/ALT more than 5xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose adjustments

dose_modifications.notes: Stop treatment
Decrease weekly dose by one level without interruption of therapy
Withhold until resolution to grade 1 or lower, then decrease by one dose level

monitoring_requirements: FBC, U&Es and LFTs on day 1, 8 and 15
special_warnings: This is an unlicensed dosage schedule.
Paclitaxel is a vesicant.

Hypersensitivity reactions tend to occur with the first or second infusion. Interrupt for minor symptoms (flushing, localised rashes) - may restart at lower rate with intensive monitoring if resolve within 5 minutes. Immediately discontinue for severe reactions (profound hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema).
Paclitaxel must be administered via a non-PVC administration set containing an in-line 0.22 micron filter.
In patients with poor bone marrow reserves consider starting at 60mg/m² with a view to increase to 80mg/m² if well tolerated.
If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.

indication: First line treatment option for people with advanced renal cell carcinoma; Second line treatment of advanced renal cell carcinoma in those intolerant of first line sunitinib; Performance status 0 or 1

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Take as a single daily dose on an empty stomach, at least one hour before or two hours after food. Swallow whole with a glass of water. Available as 200mg and 400mg tablets. 10 cycles will be set in ARIA. Treatment continued as long as clinical benefit observed or until unacceptable toxicity.

take_home_medicines.special_instructions: Take on an empty stomach, at least 1 hour before or 2 hours after food. Swallow whole with water.

dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt therapy for 7 days. Once recovered consider dose reduction in steps of 200mg.
Interrupt therapy for 7 days. Once recovered consider dose reduction in steps of 200mg.
800mg daily
200mg daily; monitor LFTs weekly; increase dose in 200mg increments as tolerated
Contra-indicated
100% dose
Continue but monitor transaminases weekly until they return to ≤3xULN or baseline
Interrupt until AST/ALT ≤3xULN or baseline. If benefit outweighs risk, reintroduce at reduced dose and measure LFTs weekly for 8 weeks. If AST/ALT return >3xULN then discontinue.
Discontinue pazopanib
100% dose
Clinical decision required; no data available

dose_modifications.condition: Persistent hypertension >140/90mmHg despite standard antihypertensive therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose in 200mg steps. If hypertension persists discontinue pazopanib.
Reduce dose by 200mg
Stop pazopanib until resolves to at least grade 2. May re-start with 200mg dose reduction.
Discontinue pazopanib

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 2 skin reaction or stomatitis
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1, then restart with 200mg dose reduction

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 skin reaction or stomatitis
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1, then restart with 200-400mg dose reduction

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Prior to each cycle. If counts fall below levels during treatment interrupt for 7 days. Once recovered consider dose reduction in steps of 200mg. If third dose reduction necessary treatment should be stopped.

monitoring_requirements: FBC and U&Es every 4 weeks
LFTs at baseline then week 3, 5, 7 and 9 then every 4 weeks and as clinically indicated
Blood pressure weekly for the first 4 weeks then every 4 weeks
ECG at baseline then every 6-12 months if patient has relevant cardiac history (e.g. on anti-arrhythmics, history of QT prolongation)
Thyroid function tests at baseline then every 3 months
Urinalysis at baseline and then every 3 months for proteinuria
special_warnings: Cases of hepatic failure (including fatalities) have been reported during use of pazopanib.
Pazopanib interacts with many medications. Always check for drug interactions.
The National Patient Safety Alert on oral chemotherapy (NPSA/2008/RRR001) must be followed in relation to pazopanib.
Pazopanib should only be prescribed under the supervision of an oncologist.
If a third dose reduction is necessary treatment should be stopped.
Hypertension should be treated initially as per NICE guidelines. Treat persistently high BP >140/90mmHg.
Hypothyroidism is common; manage according to standard medical practice, no need to reduce pazopanib dose.
Loperamide may be used for diarrhoea management.

drugs.special_instructions: Take as a single daily dose on an empty stomach, at least one hour before or two hours after food. Swallow whole with a glass of water. Available as 200mg and 400mg tablets. 10 cycles will be set in ARIA. Treatment continued as long as clinical benefit observed or until unacceptable toxicity.
drugs.notes: once daily continuously | Take as a single daily dose on an empty stomach, at least one hour before or two hours after food. Swallow whole with a glass of water. Available as 200mg and 400mg tablets. 10 cycles will be set in ARIA. Treatment continued as long as clinical benefit observed or until unacceptable toxicity.

indication: Pembrolizumab is indicated for locally advanced (T4b any N or any T N2-3 disease) or metastatic (any T any N M1 disease) transitional cell carcinoma of the urothelial tract cancer previously treated with platinum-based chemotherapy where there has been disease progression during or following previous platinum-based combination chemotherapy for inoperable locally advanced or metastatic urothelial cancer. Also indicated as first line treatment of locally advanced or metastatic urothelial cancer in patients who are ineligible for cisplatin-based chemotherapy.

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Administer using a low protein binding filter (0.2 micron). Maximum treatment duration is two years (35 cycles). Dose reductions are NOT permitted for immunotherapy. Patient must be given a pembrolizumab patient information card.

pre_medications.timing: when required for infusion related reactions
pre_medications.special_instructions: For treatment of infusion related reactions only

pre_medications.timing: when required for infusion related reactions
pre_medications.special_instructions: For treatment of infusion related reactions only

pre_medications.timing: when required for infusion related reactions
pre_medications.special_instructions: Maximum dose 4g per 24 hours. There should be 4 hours between doses.

dose_modifications.notes: Dose reductions NOT permitted. Delay or discontinue only. Treatment breaks of up to 12 weeks beyond expected cycle length allowed.
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. May re-start when liver function remains at NCI-CTC grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

dose_modifications.condition: AST/ALT ≥5xULN or 50% increase from baseline in patients with liver metastases; or bilirubin >3xULN; or not improving to grade 1 within 12 weeks; or corticosteroid cannot be reduced to ≤10mg prednisolone per day within 12 weeks; or any grade 3+ reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. Resume when reaction remains at NCI-CTC grade 1 or below following tapering.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above nephritis; or not improving to grade 1 within 12 weeks; or recurrent grade 3+ reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue

Withhold; consider pulmonary consultation. Treat with systemic corticosteroids 1-2mg/kg/day prednisone equivalent. Resume if improves to grade 0 or 1 within 12 weeks and corticosteroids reduced to ≤prednisolone 10mg/day.
Permanently discontinue
Withhold and administer corticosteroids. May re-start when colitis remains at NCI-CTC grade 1 following corticosteroid taper.

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 colitis or above; or not improving to grade 1 within 12 weeks
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above skin reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold; consider dermatology referral; systemic corticosteroids prednisolone 1mg/kg/day may be needed

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or 4 infusion related reaction
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: No specific haematological thresholds; little need to adjust dose for haematological toxicity. Maximum treatment duration 2 years (35 cycles). Current funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer. Formal medical review at least by end of 3rd cycle.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day one of each cycle
Thyroid function tests prior to starting treatment and then before each administration (cycle) or when clinically indicated
special_warnings: Dose reductions are NOT permitted for pembrolizumab. Delay or discontinue only.
Maximum treatment duration is two years (35 cycles of 21-day cycles).
Treatment breaks of up to 12 weeks beyond expected cycle length are allowed solely to allow immune toxicities to settle.
Current funding approval precludes further treatment after interruption of 12 weeks or longer.
Systemic corticosteroids before starting pembrolizumab should be avoided. Can be used after starting to treat immune-related adverse reactions.
Pembrolizumab must be given as monotherapy.
A formal medical review should occur at least by the end of the third cycle.
The patient must not have received prior anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, anti-PDL2, anti-CD137 or anti-CTLA-4 therapy (unless compassionate use programme).
Administer using a low protein binding filter (0.2 micron).

drugs.special_instructions: Administer using a low protein binding filter (0.2 micron). Maximum treatment duration is two years (35 cycles). Dose reductions are NOT permitted for immunotherapy. Patient must be given a pembrolizumab patient information card.
drugs.notes: every 21 days | Administer using a low protein binding filter (0.2 micron). Maximum treatment duration is two years (35 cycles). Dose reductions are NOT permitted for immunotherapy. Patient must be given a pembrolizumab patient information card.
